name: No witnesses to my scaffold fall in Salt Lake—what evidence do I need?
description: Photos, medicals, OSHA reports—lawyers get more via discovery.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: No witnesses to my scaffold fall in Salt Lake—what evidence do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Photos, medicals, OSHA reports—lawyers get more via discovery.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Medical records, photos, incident reports, expert reconstructions suffice even without witnesses.** Utah courts accept OSHA logs, coworker statements, site videos.[1][4] Key factors: (1) Supervisor reports often exist; (2) preserve clothing/PPE showing defects; (3) medical timeline proves causation; (4) lawyers use discovery for hidden evidence.[2] Take site photos immediately, request employer logs, avoid erasing digital proof. An attorney compels full disclosure for strong cases.
